✨ 10 Unspoken Cultural Rules to Know Before Visiting Japan 🇯🇵
📌 SAVE THIS for your Japan itinerary, these small details will completely change your travel experience

Japan travel isn’t about being perfect…
it’s about being mindful, respectful, and aware of the culture around you 🤍

Once you understand these, everything from Tokyo trains to Kyoto temples, just flows effortlessly.

🌸 Observe first, follow gently (Japan cultural etiquette)
🚆 Trains are extremely punctual → always arrive early
🤍 Respect personal space (quiet, calm environments)
🍜 Slurping noodles = appreciation (Japanese food etiquette)
👟 Shoes off indoors (homes, temples, some restaurants)
📸 Be mindful with photos (especially in shrines, temples & geisha districts)
🧘‍♀️ Silence = respect (trains, buses, public spaces)
🙏 Temples are sacred spaces (not just photo spots)
💴 Cash is still widely used in Japan
🙇‍♀️ A small bow + smile goes a long way

These aren’t strict rules 
they’re the reason Japan feels so safe, peaceful, and beautifully organized ✨

caption:
✨ The Ultimate Door County, Wisconsin Travel Guide, Things to Do for a Perfect Weekend Getaway! 🌊

If you’re planning a summer or fall trip to Door County, Wisconsin, this peninsula on Lake Michigan is Midwest paradise. From kayaking sea caves to tasting local wines, every corner feels like a hidden gem waiting to be explored 🍒

Here’s everything you can’t miss in Door County 👇

🚤 Pontoon or Boat Ride on Green Bay: Cruise through calm waters, stop by island beaches, and catch golden-hour sunsets over the bluffs 🌅

🛶 Kayak at Cave Point County Park: Paddle through Door County’s iconic limestone cliffs and sea caves, one of the best outdoor adventures in Wisconsin!

🌲 Hike in Peninsula State Park: Try Eagle Trail or Sunset Trail for jaw-dropping lake views, forest walks, and photo ops at Eagle Bluff Lighthouse.

🍷 Wine Tasting & Local Eats: Visit Door Peninsula Winery, Stone’s Throw Winery, and Lautenbach’s Orchard Country for cherry wines, local cheeses, and cozy tasting rooms.

🏖️ Relax at Egg Harbor Beach: Spend a laid-back afternoon on the sand with views of sailboats and turquoise water. Perfect picnic spot!

🎭 Skyway Drive-In Theater: End your day with nostalgic movie nights under the stars, one of the last classic drive-ins in Wisconsin 🎬

🌌 Camp Under the Stars: Whether you’re in Peninsula State Park or Newport State Park, camping here means waking up to waves, pine trees, and sunrise skies.

🍒 Cherry Picking & Farm Markets: Visit in summer to pick your own cherries, grab fresh jams, or taste Door County’s famous cherry pie 🥧

🪞 Explore Fish Creek, Ephraim & Sister Bay : These charming lake towns are full of cute boutiques, coffee shops, and waterfront dining.

📍 Why Visit Door County?
It’s one of the best weekend getaways from Chicago, Milwaukee, or Madison offering everything from outdoor adventure to small-town charm and breathtaking lakefront views.

caption:
You might still be able to see cherry blossoms in Chicago! 

📍 Where to See the Cherry Blossoms:
	•Location:
➔ Jackson Park, Chicago
➔ Near the Columbian Basin (by the Museum of Science and Industry)
➔ The main area is around Wooded Island and Columbian Basin Lagoon
➔ You can follow signs toward the Garden of the Phoenix (Japanese Garden), this area is stunning during bloom!

🚗 Where to Park:
	•Easiest Parking:
➔ Museum of Science and Industry Parking Garage (address: 5700 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60637)
➔ Then it’s a 5–10 minute walk to the cherry blossom spots near the Garden of the Phoenix and Wooded Island.
	•Street Parking:
➔ There’s limited free and metered parking around 59th Street, Midway Plaisance, and Lake Shore Drive, but it gets crowded on weekends!

•Tip:
➔ Go early morning (8–10 AM) for easier parking and fewer crowds and softer golden light for photos! ✨

•Bloom Timing:
➔ Peak bloom lasts about 5–7 days, so once they start, try to visit quickly! 🌸
➔ Watch weather forecasts windy/rainy days can knock petals off fast.

•Best Photo Spots:
➔ Along the edges of Columbian Basin Lagoon
➔ On the bridges around Wooded Island
➔ Inside the Garden of the Phoenix under the arching trees

🌸 About the Cherry Blossom Trees in Chicago (Jackson Park):
	•Chicago’s cherry blossom trees were originally a gift from Japan to symbolize friendship. 🇯🇵🤝🇺🇸
	•The trees in Jackson Park were planted near the Columbian Basin during the early 2010s to honor this relationship.
	•They don’t bloom every year because of Chicago’s harsh winters, the blossoms are sensitive and can be damaged.
	•The blooms typically peak in late April, but it’s very weather dependent, early warm spells can speed it up.

If you’re planning a dreamy trip to Lake Como between June 15 and September 15, here’s a heads-up you don’t want to miss 🧳🇮🇹

🚫 The Milan–Lecco–Sondrio train line (including the popular Varenna-Esino stop) will be suspended all summer due to construction for the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics.

🚗 Rent a car – great flexibility, just beware of narrow roads & limited parking
🛻 Hire a driver – ideal for small groups

caption:
Trains in Switzerland are magical… and expensive 💸🚞
But I’m going to help you master the art of saving money — so you can see the best of the Alps without breaking the bank. 🏔️✨

Here are the smartest hacks for affordable train travel in Switzerland:

🚆 1. Swiss Half Fare Card
Get 50% off trains, buses, boats, mountain gondolas, and cable cars. Valid for a full month, and perfect if you’re hopping between towns or heading to places like Jungfraujoch or Zermatt.

🚆 2. Super Saver Tickets
Book early on the SBB app or website (up to 6 months ahead!) for up to 50% off on specific train routes. Combine with the Half Fare Card for even more savings!

🚆 3. Swiss Travel Pass
Unlimited travel for 3–15 days on trains, buses, boats, and even free access to 500+ museums! Great for packed itineraries or scenic rides like the Glacier Express 🚠

🚆 4. Regional Passes
Staying in one area? Grab a Berner Oberland, Jungfrau, or Tell Pass — they cover trains, cable cars, and boats in specific regions and often include mountain excursions too!

🚆 5. Saver Day Pass
Buy early and travel all day for a low price — even cheaper when paired with a Half Fare Card!

🚆 6. Eurail or Interrail Pass
Traveling beyond Switzerland? This is your best bet — just double-check scenic route reservations!

🚫 Bonus Tip: Skip taxis & Ubers
Swiss trains and buses go everywhere — so you’ll save tons just using public transport.

📲 Always download the SBB Mobile app, book early, and travel off-peak for even more savings!

✨ Switzerland is pricey — but it doesn’t have to be.

caption:
Ready to add one of Arizona’s most iconic views to your bucket list? Here’s your ultimate guide to conquering Horseshoe Bend on your next Southwest road trip. 

Don’t forget to follow @journeysbysam for more epic travel inspo! ✨

📍 Location & Overview
Just 5 miles west of Page, AZ, Horseshoe Bend delivers a jaw-dropping, 1,000-ft drop to the emerald green Colorado River below. This horseshoe-shaped meander is perfect for sunrise photography, dramatic sunsets, and unbeatable panoramic views.

🥾 Hike Details
	•	Distance: 1.5 miles round-trip
	•	Difficulty: Easy–moderate, but unshaded desert terrain
	•	Time Needed: 30–45 minutes one way (plus time for photos!)
	•	Trail Tips: Wear sturdy hiking shoes, a wide-brim hat, and UV-blocking sunglasses

☀️ Best Time to Visit
	•	Sunrise (5:30–7 AM): Cool temps & minimal crowds
	•	Midday (11 AM–1 PM): Harsh light but vibrant river hues
	•	Sunset (6–8 PM): Golden hour skies & dramatic shadows

💲 Fees & Permits
	•	Parking: $10 per car | $5 per motorcycle | $35 per RV/commercial van
	•	Payment: Cash or card accepted at the automated kiosk
	•	Note: America the Beautiful or other NPS passes are not valid here!

🚻 Facilities & Safety
	•	Restrooms: Portable toilets at the lot—none on the trail
	•	Edge Safety: No railings beyond the main overlook—keep kids and pets on a short leash!
	•	Stay Hydrated: Bring at least 1 L of water per person

📷 Photo & Gear Tips
	•	Lens: Wide-angle (16–35 mm) for the full curve; telephoto (70–200 mm) for close-ups of the river
	•	Filters: Polarizer to cut glare & boost river color
	•	Drone: Strictly prohibited—stick to your camera or phone

🌵 Nearby Must-Sees
Antelope Canyon (slot-canyon wonder), Lake Powell boat tours, and the engineering marvel of Glen Canyon Dam are all within a 15-minute drive.

🌟 Pikes Peak Guide
📍 Must-See Highlights:

🚂 Pikes Peak Cog Railway – America’s highest cog train perfect if you’d rather relax and enjoy the ride!

💰 Entrance Fee:

✅ Dress in layers – it’s cold and windy at the top! 🧤
✅ Hydrate well – altitude sickness is real! 💧
✅ Book train tickets in advance – they sell out fast!

caption:
Let me tell you the chaotic truth behind my Salkantay Trek to Machu Picchu. Because no one talks about the real stuff — like nearly quitting before it even starts 😅

🗓️ Day 1:
We’re driving through the curviest roads in the Andes — like Mario Kart, but make it 13,000 ft elevation 😵‍💫
I start puking before we even hit the trailhead. Yes, before.
I skipped the iconic Humantay Lake (don’t remind me 😭), collapsed into camp like a noodle, and told my guide, “I don’t think I can do this.”

He smiled and said, “We’ll see tomorrow.”
(He knew something I didn’t.)

🗓️ Day 2:
Altitude? Still winning.
My stomach? Still not okay.
And then… the mountain appeared. The 3-hour vertical climb to Salkantay Pass (15,090 ft). I knew there was no way I was making it on foot.

Enter: my hero in hooves. 🐴✨
For 70–80 soles (less than $20 USD!), I hired a horse, hopped on like a dusty princess, and rode all the way to the base of the glacier while the other hikers were fighting for oxygen 😂

BUT PLOT TWIST:
🚨 THE HORSE ONLY TAKES YOU UP.
Not down.
So after feeling like a mountain queen… I had to hike down on foot, legs shaking, praying I wouldn’t roll all the way back to Cusco 🫠

But hey — I didn’t quit.
I made it to the top.
And honestly? That horse deserves a 5-star TripAdvisor review!!

💡 What to know before hiking Salkantay Trek (from someone who suffered):
	•	Curvy roads = take motion sickness meds BEFORE the ride
	•	Day 1 hike is no joke — but skipping Humantay doesn’t mean you failed
	•	Horses are available on Day 2 to take you up the steepest part
	•	Bring 70–80 soles in cash
	•	You’ll still need to hike down so bring trekking poles
	•	Altitude is real. Hydrate, take your pills, and eat light

caption:
Did you miss this spot??? 🚗✨

🚗✨ Pikes Peak is a Colorado classic you can’t miss — it’s one of the most iconic “14ers” in the U.S. and the drive to the summit is seriously next-level scenic! 🏔️💨

Please follow @journeysbysam for epic PTO-friendly adventures like this one! 🌍💼

🌟 Pikes Peak Guide
📍 Must-See Highlights:
🚗 Pikes Peak Highway – A winding 19-mile scenic road to the summit with jaw-dropping views all the way up!
🚂 Pikes Peak Cog Railway – America’s highest cog train—perfect if you’d rather relax and enjoy the ride!
🥾 Devil’s Playground Trail – A challenging but rewarding hike starting above the treeline with insane alpine views.
☕ Summit House – Home to the famous high-altitude donuts (yes, they taste extra magical at 14,115 ft!).
📸 Summit Views – Panoramic views stretching from the Great Plains to the Rocky Mountains. Unreal!

💰 Entrance Fee:
🚗 $15 per adult | $50 per car (up to 5 people)
🚋 Cog Railway: Tickets start around $40–$60+ per person

✨ Tips for Visiting:
✅ Go early to avoid crowds and afternoon storms ⚡
✅ Dress in layers – it’s cold and windy at the top! 🧤
✅ Hydrate well – altitude sickness is real! 💧
✅ Book train tickets in advance – they sell out fast!
✅ Take your time driving – the views are just that good 😍

This isn’t just a road trip — it’s a love letter to the California coast. 🌊✨
If you’re cruising down Highway 1, here are 10 stops you can’t miss — each one more magical than the last:

📍Bixby Creek Bridge – the most iconic photo stop on the route
📍Garrapata State Park – wildflowers, cliffs, and secret trails
📍Calla Lily Valley – springtime fairytale bloom (seasonal!)

📍McWay Falls – 80-ft waterfall straight onto the beach
📍Limekiln State Park – waterfalls, redwoods, and ocean views
📍San Simeon – elephant seals + Hearst Castle views
📍Morro Bay – boats, sea otters, and that iconic rock at sunset

Every turn on this stretch of coast feels like a movie scene — so save this for your next California adventure and let the ocean lead the way. 🚗🎶

caption:
Cusco isn’t just a city — it’s an experience. 🫶🏻

Before I dive further, follow @journeysbysam for bucket list travels! 🫶🏻

Also I have clicked all the pics on @canonusa 

But honestly? There are a few things I really wish I had known before my first day there…

Here’s your ultimate cheat sheet to make your Cusco trip even more magical (and way less stressful):

➔ Altitude is no joke — hydrate and rest!
➔ Always carry cash (coins too!).
➔ Offline maps are your best friend.
➔ Stay alert for pickpockets.
➔ Walk, wander, and get lost — the side streets are pure magic.
➔ Sunscreen isn’t optional — even in cold weather.
➔ Sip coca tea to feel better at altitude.
➔ Book Machu Picchu tickets wayyy ahead.
➔ Stay central to soak up every moment.
➔ Embrace the unexpected — Cusco has a way of surprising you. ✨
